If you hit with your constrict attack, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, and the target is grappled (escape 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier). Until this grapple ends, the targe

Naga.name: Poison Immunity
Naga.description: You are immune to poison damage and can't be poisoned.

Naga.name: Poison Affinity
Naga.description: You gain proficiency with the poisoner's kit.

Naga.name: Languages
Naga.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Naga.

Orc.name: Adrenaline Rush
Orc.description:
You can take the Dash action as a Bonus Action. When you do so, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency.
You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest.

Orc.name: Darkvision
Orc.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

Orc.name: Relentless Endurance
Orc.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Points instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Age
Orc (Ixalan).description: Orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Alignment
Orc (Ixalan).description: Most orcs lean toward chaotic alignments, and many serve on pirate ships that encourage an inclination toward evil.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Size
Orc (Ixalan).description: Orcs average over 6 feet in height, and they have strong, stocky builds. Your size is Medium.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Menacing
Orc (Ixalan).description: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Relentless Endurance
Orc (Ixalan).description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Savage Attacks
Orc (Ixalan).description: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Darkvision
Orc (Ixalan).description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Orc (Ixalan).name: Languages
Orc (Ixalan).description: You can speak, read, and write Common Trade Pidgin (if it exists in your campaign) and Orc.

Owlin.name: Size
Owlin.description: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

Owlin.name: Darkvision
Owlin.description: You can see in dim light within 120 feet of yourself as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Owlin.name: Flight
Owlin.description: Thanks to your wings, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can't use this flying speed if you're wearing medium or heavy armor.

Owlin.name: Silent Feathers
Owlin.description: You have proficiency in the Stealth skill.

Plasmoid.name: Size
Plasmoid.description: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

Plasmoid.name: Amorphous
Plasmoid.description: You can squeeze through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide, provided you are wearing and carrying nothing. You have advantage on ability checks you make to initiate or escape a grapple.

Plasmoid.name: Darkvision
Plasmoid.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of yourself as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Plasmoid.name: Hold Breath
Plasmoid.description: You can hold your breath for 1 hour.

Plasmoid.name: Natural Resilience
Plasmoid.description: You have resistance to acid and poison damage, and you have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned.

Plasmoid.name: Shape Self
Plasmoid.description:
As an action, you can reshape your body to give yourself a head, one or two arms, one or two legs, and makeshift hands and feet, or you can revert to a limbless blob. While you have a humanlike shape, you can wear clothing and armor made for a Humanoid of your size.
As a bonus action, you can extrude a pseudopod that is up to 6 inches wide and 10 feet long or reabsorb it into your body. As part of the same bonus action, you can use this pseudopod to manipulate an object, open or close a door or container, or pick up or set down a Tiny object. The pseudopod contains no sensory organs and can't

Reborn.name: Size
Reborn.description: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you gain this lineage.

Reborn.name: Ancestral Legacy
Reborn.description:
If you replace a race with this lineage, you can keep the following elements of that race: any skill proficiencies you gained from it and any climbing, flying, or swimming speed you gained from it.
If you don't keep any of those elements or you choose this lineage at character creation, you gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

Reborn.name: Deathless Nature
Reborn.description:
You have escaped death, a fact represented by the following benefits:
• You have advantage on saving throws against disease and being poisoned, and you have resistance to poison damage.
• You have advantage on death saving throws.
• You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.
• You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in an inactive, motionless state, during which you retain consciousness.

Reborn.name: Knowledge from a Past Life
Reborn.description: You temporarily remember glimpses of the past, perhaps faded memories from ages ago or a previous life. When you make an ability check that uses a skill, you can roll a d6 immediately after seeing the number on the d20 and add the number on the d6 to the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Rimekin.name: Cold Fire Magic
Rimekin.description:
You know the Ray of Frost cantrip. When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn the Ice Knife spell and the Flame Blade spell, respectively. You always have those spells prepared. You can cast each once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast these spells in this way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. When you cast Flame Blade using this trait, the spell deals Cold damage instead of Fire damage.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose the abilit

Rimekin.name: Cold Resistance
Rimekin.description: You have Resistance to Cold damage.

Rimekin.name: Darkvision
Rimekin.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Satyr.name: Ram
Satyr.description: You can use your head and horns to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals 1d6 + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

Satyr.name: Magic Resistance
Satyr.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells.

Satyr.name: Mirthful Leaps
Satyr.description: Whenever you make a long jump or a high jump, you can roll a d8 and add the number rolled to the number of feet you cover, even when making a standing jump. This extra distance costs movement as usual.

Satyr.name: Reveler
Satyr.description: As an embodiment of revelry, you have proficiency in the Performance and Persuasion skills, and you have proficiency with one musical instrument of your choice.

Sea Elf.name: Creature Type
Sea Elf.description: You are a Humanoid. You are also considered an elf for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be an elf.

Sea Elf.name: Speed
Sea Elf.description: Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

Sea Elf.name: Child of the Sea
Sea Elf.description: You can breathe air and water, and you have resistance to cold damage.

Sea Elf.name: Darkvision
Sea Elf.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Sea Elf.name: Fey Ancestry
Sea Elf.description: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

Sea Elf.name: Friend of the Sea
Sea Elf.description: Aquatic animals have an extraordinary affinity with your people. You can communicate simple ideas to any Beast that has a swimming speed. It can understand your words, though you have no special ability to understand it in return.

Sea Elf.name: Keen Senses
Sea Elf.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

Sea Elf.name: Trance
Sea Elf.description:
You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.
Whenever you finish this trance, you can gain two proficiencies that you don't have, each one with a weapon or a tool of your choice selected from the Player's Handbook. You mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory, and you retain them until you finish your next long rest.

Shadar-Kai.name: Creature Type
Shadar-Kai.description: You are a Humanoid. You are also considered an elf for any prerequisite or effect that requires you to be an elf.

Shadar-Kai.name: Blessing of the Raven Queen
Shadar-Kai.description:
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Starting at 3rd level, you also gain resistance to all damage when you teleport using this trait. The resistance lasts until the start of your next turn. During that time, you appear ghostly and translucent.

Shadar-Kai.name: Darkvision
Shadar-Kai.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Shadar-Kai.name: Fey Ancestry
Shadar-Kai.description: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the charmed condition on yourself.

Shadar-Kai.name: Keen Senses
Shadar-Kai.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

Shadar-Kai.name: Necrotic Resistance
Shadar-Kai.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

Shadar-Kai.name: Trance
Shadar-Kai.description:
You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.
Whenever you finish this trance, you can gain two proficiencies that you don't have, each one with a weapon or a tool of your choice selected from the Player's Handbook. You mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory, and you retain them until you finish your next long rest.

Shifter.name: Bestial Instincts
Shifter.description: Channeling the beast within, you have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Athletics, Intimidation, or Survival.

Shifter.name: Darkvision
Shifter.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were in dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Shifter.name: Shifting
Shifter.description:
As a bonus action, you can assume a more bestial appearance. This transformation lasts for 1 minute, until you die, or until you revert to your normal appearance as a bonus action. When you shift, you gain temporary hit points equal to 2 × your proficiency bonus. You can shift a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Whenever you shift, you gain an additional benefit based on one of the following options (choose when you select this race):
• 
• 
• 
•

Simic Hybrid.name: Age
Simic Hybrid.description: Hybrids begin their lives as adult humans, elves, or vedalken. They age at a slightly accelerated rate, so their maximum life spans are probably reduced somewhat. The Guardian Project has not been operating long enough to observe the full effect of this phenomenon.

Simic Hybrid.name: Alignment
Simic Hybrid.description: Most hybrids share the generally neutral outlook of the Simic Combine. They are more interested in scientific research and the standing of their guild than in moral or ethical questions. Those who leave the Combine, however, often do so because their philosophical outlook and alignment are more in line with a different guild's.

Simic Hybrid.name: Size
Simic Hybrid.description: Your size is Medium, within the normal range of your humanoid base race.

Simic Hybrid.name: Darkvision
Simic Hybrid.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Simic Hybrid.name: Languages
Simic Hybrid.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and your choice of Elvish or Vedalken.

Simic Hybrid.name: Animal Enhancement
Simic Hybrid.description:
Your body has been altered to incorporate certain animal characteristics. You choose one animal enhancement now and a second enhancement at 5th level.
At 1st level, choose one of the following options:
• 
• 
• 
At 5th level, your body evolves further, developing new characteristics. Choose one of the options you didn't take at 1st level, or one of the following options:
• 
• 
•

Siren.name: Alignment
Siren.description: Most sirens lean toward chaotic alignment, cherishing the freedom and independence that comes from joining a pirate crew.

Siren.name: Size
Siren.description: Sirens stand about 5 to 6 feet tall, but their bodies are slender and their bones partially hollow to facilitate their flight. Your size is Medium.

Siren.name: Siren's Song
Siren.description: You know the friends cantrip and can cast it without material components.

Siren.name: Flight
Siren.description: You have a flying speed of 30 feet. You can't use your flying speed while you wear medium or heavy armor. (If your campaign uses the variant rule for encumbrance, you can't use your flying speed if you are encumbered.)

Siren.name: Languages
Siren.description: You can speak, read, and write Common Trade Pidgin (if it exists in your campaign) and Siren.

Skeleton.name: Brittle Bones
Skeleton.description: You are vulnerable to bludgeoning damage.

Skeleton.name: Undead Nature
Skeleton.description: You are immune to poison damage and exhaustion, and you can't be poisoned. You don't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

Skeleton.name: Darkvision
Skeleton.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Skeleton.name: Languages
Skeleton.description: You can't speak, but you can understand the languages you knew in life.

Tabaxi.name: Age
Tabaxi.description: Tabaxi have lifespans equivalent to humans.

Tabaxi.name: Size
Tabaxi.description: Tabaxi are taller on average than humans and relatively slender. Your size is Medium.

Tabaxi.name: Darkvision
Tabaxi.description: You have a cat's keen senses, especially in the dark. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Tabaxi.name: Feline Agility
Tabaxi.description: Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your turn in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.

Tabaxi.name: Cat's Claws
Tabaxi.description: Because of your claws, you have a climbing speed of 20 feet. In addition, your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

Tabaxi.name: Cat's Talents
Tabaxi.description: You have proficiency in the Perception and Stealth skills.

Tabaxi.name: Languages
Tabaxi.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

Thri-kreen.name: Size
Thri-kreen.description: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

Thri-kreen.name: Chameleon Carapace
Thri-kreen.description:
While you aren't wearing armor, your carapace gives you a base Armor Class of 13 + your Dexterity modifier.
As an action, you can change the color of your carapace to match the color and texture of your surroundings, giving you advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in those surroundings.

Thri-kreen.name: Darkvision
Thri-kreen.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of yourself as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Thri-kreen.name: Secondary Arms
Thri-kreen.description: You have two slightly smaller secondary arms below your primary pair of arms. The secondary arms can manipulate an object, open or close a door or container, pick up or set down a Tiny object, or wield a weapon that has the L property.

Thri-kreen.name: Sleepless
Thri-kreen.description: You do not require sleep and can remain conscious during a long rest, though you must still refrain from strenuous activity to gain the benefit of the rest.

Thri-kreen.name: Thri-kreen Telepathy
Thri-kreen.description: Without the assistance of magic, you can't speak the non-thri-kreen languages you know. Instead you use telepathy to convey your thoughts. You have the magical ability to transmit your thoughts mentally to willing creatures within 120 feet of yourself. A contacted creature doesn't need to share a language with you to understand your thoughts, but it must be able to understand at least one language. Your telepathic link to a creature is broken if you and the creature move more than 120 feet apart, if either of you is incapacitated, or if either of you mentally breaks the contact (no action requ

Tiefling.name: Darkvision
Tiefling.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Tiefling.name: Fiendish Legacy
Tiefling.description:
You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a legacy from the Fiendish Legacies table. You gain the level 1 benefit of the chosen legacy.
When you reach character levels 3 and 5, you learn a higher-level spell, as shown on the table. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cas

Tiefling.name: Otherworldly Presence
Tiefling.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you use for your Fiendish Legacy Trait.

Tortle.name: Age
Tortle.description: Young tortles crawl for a few weeks after birth before learning to walk on two legs. They reach adulthood by the age of 15 and live an average of 50 years.

Tortle.name: Alignment
Tortle.description: Tortles tend to lead orderly, ritualistic lives. They develop customs and routines, becoming more set in their ways as they age. Most are lawful good. A few can be selfish and greedy, tending more toward evil, but it's unusual for a tortle to shuck off order in favor of chaos.

Tortle.name: Size
Tortle.description: Tortle adults stand 5 to 6 feet tall and average 450 pounds. Their shells account for roughly one-third of their weight. Your size is Medium.

Tortle.name: Claws
Tortle.description: Your claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

Tortle.name: Hold Breath
Tortle.description: You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour at a time. Tortles aren't natural swimmers, but they can remain underwater for some time before needing to come up for air.

Tortle.name: Natural Armor
Tortle.description: Due to your shell and the shape of your body, you are ill-suited to wearing armor. Your shell provides ample protection, however; it gives you a base AC of 17 (your Dexterity modifier doesn't affect this number). You gain no benefit from wearing armor, but if you are using a shield, you can apply the shield's bonus as normal.

Tortle.name: Shell Defense
Tortle.description: You can withdraw into your shell as an action. Until you emerge, you gain a +4 bonus to AC, and you have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws. While in your shell, you are prone, your speed is 0 and can't increase, you have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, you can't take reactions, and the only action you can take is a bonus action to emerge from your shell.

Tortle.name: Survival Instinct
Tortle.description: You gain proficiency in the Survival skill. Tortles have finely honed survival instincts.

Tortle.name: Languages
Tortle.description: You can speak, read, and write Aquan and Common.

Triton.name: Age
Triton.description: Tritons reach maturity around age 15 and can live up to 200 years.

Triton.name: Size
Triton.description: Tritons are slightly shorter than humans, averaging about 5 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

Triton.name: Swim Speed
Triton.description: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet.

Triton.name: Amphibious
Triton.description: You can breathe air and water.

Triton.name: Control Air and Water
Triton.description: A child of the sea, you can call on the magic of elemental air and water. You can cast fog cloud with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast gust of wind with it, and starting at 5th level, you can also cast wall of water with it. Once you cast a spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Triton.name: Darkvision
Triton.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Triton.name: Emissary of the Sea
Triton.description: Aquatic beasts have an extraordinary affinity with your people. You can communicate simple ideas with beasts that can breathe water. They can understand the meaning of your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return.

Triton.name: Guardians of the Depths
Triton.description: Adapted to even the most extreme ocean depths, you have resistance to cold damage.

Triton.name: Languages
Triton.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial.

Troglodyte.name: Chameleon Skin
Troglodyte.description: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

Troglodyte.name: Stench
Troglodyte.description: Any creature other than a troglodyte that starts its turn within 5 ft. of you must succeed on a 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of the creature's next turn. On a successful saving throw, the creature is immune to the stench of all troglodytes for 1 hour.

Troglodyte.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
Troglodyte.description: While in sunlight, you have disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

Troglodyte.name: Natural Armor
Troglodyte.description: Your thick hide grants you a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

Troglodyte.name: Darkvision
Troglodyte.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Troglodyte.name: Languages
Troglodyte.description: You can speak, read, and write Troglodyte.

Vampire.name: Age
Vampire.description: Vampires don't mature and age in the same way that other races do. Every living vampire is either a bloodchief, infected by Ulamog's influence in the distant reaches of history, or was spawned by a bloodchief from a living human. Most vampires are thus very old, but few have any memory of their earliest years.

Vampire.name: Alignment
Vampire.description: Vampires have no innate tendency toward evil, but consuming the life energy of other creatures often pushes them to that end. Regardless of their moral bent, the strict hierarchies of their bloodchiefs inclines them toward a lawful alignment.

Vampire.name: Size
Vampire.description: Vampires are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.

Vampire.name: Blood Thirst
Vampire.description: You can drain blood and life energy from a willing creature, or one that is grappled by you, incapacitated, or restrained. Make a melee attack against the target. If you hit, you deal 1 piercing damage and 1d6 necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken, and you regain hit points equal to that amount. The reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest. The target dies if this effect reduces its hit point maximum to 0.

Vampire.name: Darkvision
Vampire.description: Thanks to your heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Vampire.name: Languages
Vampire.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Vampire.

Vampire.name: Vampiric Resistance
Vampire.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

Vedalken.name: Age
Vedalken.description: Vedalken mature at the same rate humans do, and most are expected to settle down into an adult life by around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.

Vedalken.name: Alignment
Vedalken.description: Vedalken are most often lawful and rarely evil.

Vedalken.name: Size
Vedalken.description: Vedalken are taller than humans but more slender. They average 6 to 6½ feet tall, but usually weigh less than 200 pounds. Your size is Medium.

Vedalken.name: Languages
Vedalken.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Vedalken. The Vedalken language is renowned for its technical treatises and its catalogs of knowledge about the natural world and the aether that pervades it.

Vedalken.name: Vedalken Cunning
Vedalken.description: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

Vedalken.name: Aether Lore
Vedalken.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items or aether-powered technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.

Verdan.name: Age
Verdan.description: Verdan reach adulthood at around the age of 24, and it is thought that they might live to nearly 200 years old. However, because no verdan has died of old age since the race's initial creation, their upper age limits remain subject to speculation.

Verdan.name: Alignment
Verdan.description: Verdan are generally good, although their absence of racial identity and shared history can sometimes see individual verdan become untethered from any moral or ethical framework.

Verdan.name: Size
Verdan.description: Verdan start out similar in size to the goblins they were created from, ranging from 3 to 4 feet in height. But at some point after reaching maturity, each verdan undergoes a sudden growth spurt of 2 feet or more. At 1st level, you are a Small creature. When you reach 5th level, you become a Medium creature.

Verdan.name: Black Blood Healing
Verdan.description: The black blood that is a sign of your people's connection to That-Which-Endures boosts your natural healing. When you roll a 1 or 2 on any Hit Die you spend at the end of a short rest, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

Verdan.name: Limited Telepathy
Verdan.description: You can telepathically speak to any creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathy, but it must be able to understand at least one language. This process of communication is slow and limited, allowing you to transmit and receive only simple ideas and straightforward concepts.

Verdan.name: Persuasive
Verdan.description: Your people's lack of history makes you trustworthy and humble. You have proficiency in the Persuasion skill.

Verdan.name: Telepathic Insight
Verdan.description: Your mind's connection to the world around you strengthens your will. You have advantage on all Wisdom and Charisma saving throws.

Verdan.name: Language
Verdan.description: You speak, read, and write Common, Goblin, and one additional language of your choice. This language typically has some connection to one of the areas or cultures that has been part of your life.

Warforged.name: Age
Warforged.description: A typical warforged is between two and thirty years old. The maximum warforged lifespan remains a mystery; so far, warforged have shown no signs of deterioration due to age. You are immune to magical aging effects.

Warforged.name: Alignment
Warforged.description: Most warforged take comfort in order and discipline, tending toward law and neutrality. But some have absorbed the morality, or lack thereof, of the beings with which they served.

Warforged.name: Constructed Resilience
Warforged.description:
You were created to have remarkable fortitude, represented by the following benefits:
• You have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and you have resistance to poison damage.
• You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.
• You are immune to disease.
• You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep.

Warforged.name: Sentry's Rest
Warforged.description: When you take a long rest, you must spend at least six hours in an inactive, motionless state, rather than sleeping. In this state, you appear inert, but it doesn't render you unconscious, and you can see and hear as normal.

Warforged.name: Integrated Protection
Warforged.description:
Your body has built-in defensive layers, which can be enhanced with armor:
• You gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.
• You can don only armor with which you have proficiency. To don armor other than a shield, you must incorporate it into your body over the course of 1 hour, during which you remain in contact with the armor. To doff armor, you must spend 1 hour removing it. You can rest while donning or doffing armor in this way.
• While you live, the armor incorporated into your body can't be removed against your will.

Warforged.name: Specialized Design
Warforged.description: You gain one skill proficiency and one tool proficiency of your choice.

Warforged.name: Languages
Warforged.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

Yuan-Ti.name: Size
Yuan-Ti.description: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

Yuan-Ti.name: Darkvision
Yuan-Ti.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

Yuan-Ti.name: Magic Resistance
Yuan-Ti.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells.

Yuan-Ti.name: Poison Resilience
Yuan-Ti.description: You have advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the poisoned condition on yourself. You also have resistance to poison damage.

Yuan-Ti.name: Serpentine Spellcasting
Yuan-Ti.description:
You know the poison spray cantrip. You can cast animal friendship an unlimited number of times with this trait, but you can target only snakes with it. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast suggestion with this trait. Once you cast it, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race).

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Age
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: Purebloods mature at the same rate as humans and have lifespans similar in length to theirs.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Size
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: Purebloods match humans in average size and weight. Your size is Medium.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Darkvision
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Innate Spellcasting
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: You know the poison spray cantrip. You can cast animal friendship an unlimited number of times with this trait, but you can target only snakes with it. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast suggestion with this trait. Once you cast it, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Magic Resistance
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Poison Immunity
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: You are immune to poison damage and the poisoned condition.

Yuan-ti Pureblood.name: Languages
Yuan-ti Pureblood.description: You can speak, read, and write Common, Abyssal, and Draconic.

Zombie.name: Undead Fortitude
Zombie.description: If damage reduces you to 0 hit points, you must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5+the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, you drop to 1 hit point instead.

Zombie.name: Undead Nature
Zombie.description: You are immune to poison damage, and you can't be poisoned. You don't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

Zombie.name: Darkvision
Zombie.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Zombie.name: Languages
Zombie.description: You can't speak, but you can understand the languages you knew in life.

Fallen (Aasimar).name: Necrotic Shroud
Fallen (Aasimar).description:
Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash the divine energy within yourself, causing your eyes to turn into pools of darkness and two skeletal, ghostly, flightless wings to sprout from your back. The instant you transform, other creatures within 10 feet of you that can see you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or become frightened of you until the end of your next turn.
Your transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it as a bonus action. During it, once on each of your turns, you can deal extra necrotic damage

Protector (Aasimar).name: Radiant Soul
Protector (Aasimar).description:
Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash the divine energy within yourself, causing your eyes to glimmer and two luminous, incorporeal wings to sprout from your back.
Your transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it as a bonus action. During it, you have a flying speed of 30 feet, and once on each of your turns, you can deal extra radiant damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra radiant damage equals your level.
Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

Scourge (Aasimar).name: Radiant Consumption
Scourge (Aasimar).description:
Starting at 3rd level, you can use your action to unleash the divine energy within yourself, causing a searing light to radiate from you, pour out of your eyes and mouth, and threaten to char you.
Your transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it as a bonus action. During it, you shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, you and each creature within 10 feet of you take radiant damage equal to half your level (rounded up). In addition, once on each of your turns, you can deal extra radiant damage to one target w

Hawk-Headed (Aven).name: Hawkeyed
Hawk-Headed (Aven).description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill. In addition, attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged weapon attack rolls.

Ibis-Headed (Aven).name: Kefnet's Blessing
Ibis-Headed (Aven).description: You can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any Intelligence check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

Draconblood (Dragonborn).name: Darkvision
Draconblood (Dragonborn).description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Draconblood (Dragonborn).name: Forceful Presence
Draconblood (Dragonborn).description: You can use your understanding of creative diplomacy or intimidation to guide a conversation in your favor. When you make a Charisma (Intimidation or Persuasion) check, you can do so with advantage. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Draconblood (Dragonborn).name: Draconic Ancestry
Draconblood (Dragonborn).description:
You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon is determined by the dragon type, as shown in the table.
[tabla]

Ravenite (Dragonborn).name: Darkvision
Ravenite (Dragonborn).description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Ravenite (Dragonborn).name: Vengeful Assault
Ravenite (Dragonborn).description: When you take damage from a creature in range of a weapon you are wielding, you can use your reaction to make an attack with the weapon against that creature. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

Ravenite (Dragonborn).name: Draconic Ancestry
Ravenite (Dragonborn).description:
You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon is determined by the dragon type, as shown in the table.
[tabla]

Duergar (Dwarf).name: Superior Darkvision
Duergar (Dwarf).description: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Duergar (Dwarf).name: Duergar Resilience
Duergar (Dwarf).description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage. You also have advantage on saving throws against illusions and against being charmed or paralyzed.

Duergar (Dwarf).name: Languages
Duergar (Dwarf).description: You can speak, read, and write Common, Dwarvish, and Undercommon.

Duergar (Dwarf).name: Duergar Magic
Duergar (Dwarf).description: When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the Enlarge/Reduce spell on yourself once with this trait, using only the spell's enlarge option. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the Invisibility spell on yourself once with this trait. You don't need material components for either spell, and you can't cast them while you're in direct sunlight, although sunlight has no effect on them once cast. You regain the ability to cast these spells with this trait when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Duergar (Dwarf).name: Sunlight Sensitivity
Duergar (Dwarf).description: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

Hill (Dwarf).name: Dwarven Toughness
Hill (Dwarf).description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

Mark of Warding (Dwarf).name: Warder's Intuition
Mark of Warding (Dwarf).description: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation) check or an ability check using thieves' tools, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Warding (Dwarf).name: Wards and Seals
Mark of Warding (Dwarf).description: You can cast the alarm and mage armor spells with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast the arcane lock spell with it. Once you cast any of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you don't need material components for them when you cast them with this trait.

Mark of Warding (Dwarf).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Warding (Dwarf).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Warding Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Mountain (Dwarf).name: Dwarven Armor Training
Mountain (Dwarf).description: You have proficiency with light and medium armor.

Drow (Elf).name: Superior Darkvision
Drow (Elf).description: Accustomed to the depths of the Underdark, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Drow (Elf).name: Sunlight Sensitivity
Drow (Elf).description: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

Drow (Elf).name: Drow Magic
Drow (Elf).description: You know the dancing lights cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the faerie fire spell once with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can also cast the darkness spell once per day with this trait; you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Drow (Elf).name: Drow Weapon Training
Drow (Elf).description: You have proficiency with rapier, shortsword, and hand crossbow.

Eladrin (Elf).name: Fey Step
Eladrin (Elf).description:
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
When you reach 3rd level, your Fey Step gains an additional effect based on your season; if the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier:
• 
• 
• 
•

High (Elf).name: Elf Weapon Training
High (Elf).description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

High (Elf).name: Cantrip
High (Elf).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

High (Elf).name: Extra Language
High (Elf).description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choosing.

Mark of Shadow (Elf).name: Cunning Intuition
Mark of Shadow (Elf).description: When you make a Charisma (Performance) or Dexterity (Stealth) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Shadow (Elf).name: Shape Shadows
Mark of Shadow (Elf).description: You know the minor illusion cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the invisibility spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Shadow (Elf).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Shadow (Elf).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Shadow Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Pallid (Elf).name: Incisive Sense
Pallid (Elf).description: You have advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Insight) checks.

Pallid (Elf).name: Blessing of the Moon Weaver
Pallid (Elf).description: You know the light cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the sleep spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the invisibility spell (targeting yourself only) once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Casting these spells with this trait doesn't require material components. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Sea (Elf).name: Sea Elf Training
Sea (Elf).description: You have proficiency with the spear, trident, light crossbow, and net.

Sea (Elf).name: Child of the Sea
Sea (Elf).description: You have a swimming speed of 30 feet, and you can breathe air and water.

Sea (Elf).name: Friend of the Sea
Sea (Elf).description: Using gestures and sounds, you can communicate simple ideas with any beast that has an innate swimming speed.

Sea (Elf).name: Extra Language
Sea (Elf).description: You can speak, read, and write Aquan.

Shadar-kai (Elf).name: Necrotic Resistance
Shadar-kai (Elf).description: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

Shadar-kai (Elf).name: Blessing of the Raven Queen
Shadar-kai (Elf).description:
As a bonus action, you can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Starting at 3rd level, you also gain resistance to all damage when you teleport using this trait. The resistance lasts until the start of your next turn. During that time, you appear ghostly and translucent.

Wood (Elf).name: Elf Weapon Training
Wood (Elf).description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

Wood (Elf).name: Fleet of Foot
Wood (Elf).description: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

Wood (Elf).name: Mask of the Wild
Wood (Elf).description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

Bishatar and Tirahar (Elf (Kaladesh)).name: Fleet of Foot
Bishatar and Tirahar (Elf (Kaladesh)).description: Your ground speed increases to 35 feet.

Bishatar and Tirahar (Elf (Kaladesh)).name: Mask of the Wild
Bishatar and Tirahar (Elf (Kaladesh)).description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

Vahadar (Elf (Kaladesh)).name: Cantrip
Vahadar (Elf (Kaladesh)).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

Vahadar (Elf (Kaladesh)).name: Extra Language
Vahadar (Elf (Kaladesh)).description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choosing.

Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Mask of the Wild
Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Elf Weapon Training
Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Fleet of Foot
Joraga Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: Your ground speed increases to 35 feet.

Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Mul Daya Magic
Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You know the chill touch cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the hex spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the darkness spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Superior Darkvision
Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: Your Darkvision has a radius of 120 feet.

Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Sunlight Sensitivity
Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Elf Weapon Training
Mul Daya Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

Tajuru Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).name: Skill Versatility
Tajuru Nation (Elf (Zendikar)).description: You have proficiency with any combination of two other skills or tools of your choice.

Air (Genasi).name: Unending Breath
Air (Genasi).description: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not incapacitated.

Air (Genasi).name: Lightning Resistance
Air (Genasi).description: You have resistance to lightning damage.

Air (Genasi).name: Mingle with the Wind
Air (Genasi).description:
You know the shocking grasp cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the feather fall spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Starting 5th level, you can also cast the levitate spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast feather fall or levitate with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this

Earth (Genasi).name: Earth Walk
Earth (Genasi).description: You can move across difficult terrain without expending extra movement if you are using your walking speed on the ground or a floor.

Earth (Genasi).name: Merge with Stone
Earth (Genasi).description:
You know the blade ward cantrip. You can cast it as normal, and you can also cast it as a bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Starting at 5th level, you can cast the pass without trace spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast that spell with this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of 2nd level or higher.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast the

Fire (Genasi).name: Fire Resistance
Fire (Genasi).description: You have resistance to fire damage.

Fire (Genasi).name: Reach to the Blaze
Fire (Genasi).description:
You know the produce flame cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the burning hands spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the flame blade spell with this trait, without a material component. Once you cast burning hands or flame blade with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this race)

Water (Genasi).name: Speed
Water (Genasi).description: Your walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

Water (Genasi).name: Acid Resistance
Water (Genasi).description: You have resistance to acid damage.

Water (Genasi).name: Call to the Wave
Water (Genasi).description:
You know the acid splash cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the create or destroy water spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you can also cast the water walk spell with this trait, without requiring a material component. Once you cast create or destroy water or water walk with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. You can also cast either of those spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose

Githyanki (Gith).name: Alignment
Githyanki (Gith).description: Githyanki tend toward lawful evil. They are aggressive and arrogant, and they remain the faithful servants of their lich-queen, Vlaakith. Renegade githyanki tend toward chaos.

Githyanki (Gith).name: Decadent Mastery
Githyanki (Gith).description: You learn one language of your choice, and you are proficient with one skill or tool of your choice. In the timeless city of Tu'narath, githyanki have bountiful time to master odd bits of knowledge.

Githyanki (Gith).name: Martial Prodigy
Githyanki (Gith).description: You are proficient with light and medium armor and with shortsword, longsword, and greatsword.

Githyanki (Gith).name: Githyanki Psionics
Githyanki (Gith).description:
You know the mage hand cantrip, and the hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait.
When you reach 3rd level, you can cast jump once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the misty step spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells. When you cast them with this trait, they don't require components.

Githzerai (Gith).name: Alignment
Githzerai (Gith).description: Githzerai tend toward lawful neutral. Their rigorous training in psychic abilities requires an implacable mental discipline.

Githzerai (Gith).name: Mental Discipline
Githzerai (Gith).description: You have advantage on saving throws against the charmed and frightened conditions. Under the tutelage of monastic masters, githzerai learn to govern their own minds.

Githzerai (Gith).name: Githzerai Psionics
Githzerai (Gith).description:
You know the mage hand cantrip, and the hand is invisible when you cast the cantrip with this trait.
When you reach 3rd level, you can cast shield once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the detect thoughts spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.
Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells. When you cast them with this trait, they don't require components.

Deep (Gnome).name: Age
Deep (Gnome).description: Deep gnomes are short-lived for gnomes. They mature at the same rate humans do and are considered full-grown adults by 25. They live 200 to 250 years, although hard toil and the dangers of the Underdark often claim them before their time.

Deep (Gnome).name: Size
Deep (Gnome).description: Unlike other gnomes, svirfneblin tend to weigh 80 to 120 pounds. Your size is Small.

Deep (Gnome).name: Alignment
Deep (Gnome).description: Svirfneblin believe that survival depends on avoiding entanglements with other creatures and not making enemies, so they favor neutral alignments. They rarely wish others ill, and they are unlikely to take risks on behalf of others, except those dearest to them.

Deep (Gnome).name: Superior Darkvision
Deep (Gnome).description: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Deep (Gnome).name: Stone Camouflage
Deep (Gnome).description: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide in rocky terrain.

Deep (Gnome).name: Languages
Deep (Gnome).description: You can speak, read, and write Common, Gnomish, and Undercommon.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Age
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: Deep gnomes are short-lived for gnomes. They mature at the same rate humans do and are considered full-grown adults by 25. They live 200 to 250 years, although hard toil and the dangers of the Underdark often claim them before their time.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Alignment
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: Svirfneblin believe that survival depends on avoiding entanglements with other creatures and not making enemies, so they favor neutral alignments. They rarely wish others ill, and they are unlikely to take risks on behalf of others.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Size
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: A typical svirfneblin stands about 3 to 3½ feet tall and weighs 80 to 120 pounds. Your size is Small.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Superior Darkvision
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Stone Camouflage
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide in rocky terrain.

Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).name: Languages
Deep/Svirfneblin (Gnome).description: You can speak, read, and write Common, Gnomish, and Undercommon. The svirfneblin dialect is more guttural than surface Gnomish, and most svirfneblin know only a little bit of Common, but those who deal with outsiders (and that includes you as an adventurer) pick up enough Common to get by in other lands.

Forest (Gnome).name: Natural Illusionist
Forest (Gnome).description: You know the minor illusion cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

Forest (Gnome).name: Speak with Small Beasts
Forest (Gnome).description: Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Small or smaller beasts. Forest gnomes love animals and often keep squirrels, badgers, rabbits, moles, woodpeckers, and other creatures as beloved pets.

Mark of Scribing (Gnome).name: Gifted Scribe
Mark of Scribing (Gnome).description: When you make an Intelligence (History) check or an ability check using calligrapher's supplies, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Scribing (Gnome).name: Scribe's Insight
Mark of Scribing (Gnome).description: You know the message cantrip. You can also cast comprehend languages once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a short or long rest. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the magic mouth spell with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Scribing (Gnome).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Scribing (Gnome).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Scribing Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Rock (Gnome).name: Artificer's Lore
Rock (Gnome).description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus, instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.

Rock (Gnome).name: Tinker
Rock (Gnome).description:
You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). The device ceases to function after 24 hours (unless you spend 1 hour repairing it to keep the device functioning), or when you use your action to dismantle it; at that time, you can reclaim the materials used to create it. You can have up to three such devices active at a time.
When you create a device, choose one of the following options:
Clockwork Toy: This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person, such as a

Ixalan (Goblin).name: Alignment
Ixalan (Goblin).description: Most goblins are wildly chaotic, with no particular inclination toward good or evil but a strong tendency toward mischief.

Ixalan (Goblin).name: Agile Climber
Ixalan (Goblin).description: You have a climbing speed of 25 feet. You can't use your climbing speed while you wear medium or heavy armor. (If your campaign uses the variant rule for encumbrance, you can't use your climbing speed if you are encumbered.)

Ixalan (Goblin).name: Languages
Ixalan (Goblin).description: You can speak, read, and write Common Trade Pidgin (if it exists in your campaign) and Goblin.

Zendikar; Grotag Tribe (Goblin).name: Grotag Tamer
Zendikar; Grotag Tribe (Goblin).description: You have proficiency in the Animal Handling skill.

Zendikar; Lavastep Tribe (Goblin).name: Lavastep Grit
Zendikar; Lavastep Tribe (Goblin).description: You have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in rocky or subterranean environments.

Zendikar; Tuktuk Tribe (Goblin).name: Tuktuk Cunning
Zendikar; Tuktuk Tribe (Goblin).description: You have proficiency with thieves' tools.

Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).name: Deductive Intuition
Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).description: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation) or a Wisdom (Insight) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).name: Magical Detection
Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).description: You can cast the detect magic and detect poison and disease spells with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast the see invisibility spell with it. Once you cast any of these spells with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you don't require material components for them.

Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).name: Spells of the Mark
Variant; Mark of Detection (Half-Elf).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Detection Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).name: Windwright's Intuition
Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).description: When you make a Dexterity (Acrobatics) check or any ability check involving navigator's tools, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).name: Storm's Boon
Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).description: You have resistance to lightning damage.

Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).name: Headwinds
Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).description: You know the gust cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the gust of wind spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).name: Spells of the Mark
Variant; Mark of Storm (Half-Elf).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Storm Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).name: Darkvision
Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).name: Hunter's Intuition
Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).description: When you make a Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).name: Finder's Magic
Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).description: You can cast the hunter's mark spell with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast the locate object spell with it. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).name: Languages
Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).name: Spells of the Mark
Variant; Mark of Finding (Half-Orc).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Finding Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Ghostwise (Halfling).name: Silent Speech
Ghostwise (Halfling).description: You can speak telepathically to any creature within 30 feet of you. The creature understands you only if the two of you share a language. You can speak telepathically in this way to one creature at a time.

Lightfoot (Halfling).name: Naturally Stealthy
Lightfoot (Halfling).description: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

Lotusden (Halfling).name: Child of the Wood
Lotusden (Halfling).description: You know the druidcraft cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the entangle spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the spike growth spell once with this trait and regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. Casting these spells with this trait doesn't require material components. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Lotusden (Halfling).name: Timberwalk
Lotusden (Halfling).description: Ability checks made to track you have disadvantage, and you can move across difficult terrain made of nonmagical plants and undergrowth without expending extra movement.

Mark of Healing (Halfling).name: Medical Intuition
Mark of Healing (Halfling).description: When you make a Wisdom (Medicine) check or an ability check using an herbalism kit, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Healing (Halfling).name: Healing Touch
Mark of Healing (Halfling).description: You can cast the cure wounds spell with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast lesser restoration with it. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Healing (Halfling).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Healing (Halfling).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Healing Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).name: Ever Hospitable
Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).description: When you make a Charisma (Persuasion) check or an ability check involving brewer's supplies or cook's utensils, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).name: Innkeeper's Magic
Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).description: You know the prestidigitation cantrip. You can also cast the purify food and drink and unseen servant spells with this trait. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Hospitality (Halfling).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Hospitality Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Stout (Halfling).name: Stout Resilience
Stout (Halfling).description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

Keldon (Human).name: Age
Keldon (Human).description: Keldons reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.

Keldon (Human).name: Alignment
Keldon (Human).description: Keldons tend toward chaotic alignments, and many walk a fine line between good and evil.

Keldon (Human).name: Size
Keldon (Human).description: Keldons are taller and heavier than the human norms of other cultures, standing almost universally above 6 feet tall and reaching heights above 7 feet. Your size is Medium.

Keldon (Human).name: Languages
Keldon (Human).description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Keldon.

Keldon (Human).name: Natural Athlete
Keldon (Human).description: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

Keldon (Human).name: Keldon Resilience
Keldon (Human).description: You have proficiency in Strength saving throws.

Keldon (Human).name: Icehaven Born
Keldon (Human).description: You are naturally adapted to Extreme Cold, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

Mark of Handling (Human).name: Wild Intuition
Mark of Handling (Human).description: When you make a Wisdom (Animal Handling) or Intelligence (Nature) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Handling (Human).name: Primal Connection
Mark of Handling (Human).description: You can cast the animal friendship and speak with animals spells with this trait, requiring no material component. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a short or long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Handling (Human).name: The Bigger They Are
Mark of Handling (Human).description: Starting at 3rd level, you can target a beast or monstrosity when you cast animal friendship or speak with animals, provided the creature's Intelligence score is 3 or lower.

Mark of Handling (Human).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Handling (Human).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Handling Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Mark of Making (Human).name: Artisan's Intuition
Mark of Making (Human).description: When you make an Arcana check or an ability check involving artisan's tools, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Making (Human).name: Maker's Gift
Mark of Making (Human).description: You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

Mark of Making (Human).name: Spellsmith
Mark of Making (Human).description: You know the mending cantrip. You can also cast the magic weapon spell with this trait. When you do so, the spell lasts for 1 hour and doesn't require concentration. Once you cast the spell with this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Mark of Making (Human).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Making (Human).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Making Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Mark of Passage (Human).name: Courier's Speed
Mark of Passage (Human).description: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

Mark of Passage (Human).name: Intuitive Motion
Mark of Passage (Human).description: When you make a Dexterity (Acrobatics) check or any ability check to operate or maintain a land vehicle, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Passage (Human).name: Magical Passage
Mark of Passage (Human).description: You can cast the misty step spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it when you finish a long rest. Dexterity is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

Mark of Passage (Human).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Passage (Human).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Passage Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Mark of Sentinel (Human).name: Sentinel's Intuition
Mark of Sentinel (Human).description: When you make a Wisdom (Insight) or Wisdom (Perception) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Mark of Sentinel (Human).name: Guardian's Shield
Mark of Sentinel (Human).description: You can cast the shield spell once with this trait, and you regain the ability to cast it after you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

Mark of Sentinel (Human).name: Vigilant Guardian
Mark of Sentinel (Human).description: When a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to swap places with that creature, and you are hit by the attack instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Mark of Sentinel (Human).name: Spells of the Mark
Mark of Sentinel (Human).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Sentinel Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Variant (Human).name: Skills
Variant (Human).description: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

Variant (Human).name: Feat
Variant (Human).description: You gain one feat of your choice.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).name: Darkvision
Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).name: Hunter's Intuition
Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).description: When you make a Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the ability check.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).name: Finder's Magic
Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).description: You can cast the hunter's mark spell with this trait. Starting at 3rd level, you can also cast the locate object spell with it. Once you cast either spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).name: Languages
Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Goblin.

Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).name: Spells of the Mark
Variant; Mark of Finding (Human).description:
If you have the Spellcasting or the Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Finding Spells table are added to the spell list of your spellcasting class.
[tabla]

Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).name: Forest Folk
Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).description: You have proficiency in the Survival skill.

Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).name: Fleet of Foot
Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).description: Your base walking speed is 40 feet.

Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).name: Sure-footed
Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).description: When you use the Dash action, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement on that turn.

Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).name: Spring Attack
Kessig (Human (Innistrad)).description: When you make a melee attack against a creature, you don't provoke opportunity attacks from that creature for the rest of your turn, whether you hit or not.

Nephalia (Human (Innistrad)).name: Breadth of Knowledge
Nephalia (Human (Innistrad)).description: You gain proficiency in any combination of four skills or with four tools of your choice.

Stensia (Human (Innistrad)).name: Daunting
Stensia (Human (Innistrad)).description: You have proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

Stensia (Human (Innistrad)).name: Tough
Stensia (Human (Innistrad)).description: Your hit point maximum increases by 2, and it increases by 2 every time you gain a level.

Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).name: Cantrip
Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).name: Lore of the Waters
Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).description: You gain proficiency in the History and Nature skills.

Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).name: Languages
Ixalan; Blue (Merfolk).description: You can speak, read, and write Common Trade Pidgin (if it exists in your campaign), Merfolk, and one additional language of your choice.

Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).name: Cantrip
Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).name: Mask of the Wild
Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).name: Languages
Ixalan; Green (Merfolk).description: You can speak, read, and write Common Trade Pidgin (if it exists in your campaign), Merfolk, and one additional language of your choice.

Zendikar; Cosi Creed (Merfolk).name: Cantrip
Zendikar; Cosi Creed (Merfolk).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the bard spell list. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

Zendikar; Cosi Creed (Merfolk).name: Creed of the Trickster
Zendikar; Cosi Creed (Merfolk).description: You have proficiency in the Sleight of Hand and Stealth skills.

Zendikar; Emeria Creed (Merfolk).name: Cantrip
Zendikar; Emeria Creed (Merfolk).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

Zendikar; Emeria Creed (Merfolk).name: Wind Creed Manipulation
Zendikar; Emeria Creed (Merfolk).description: You have proficiency in the Deception and Persuasion skills.

Zendikar; Ula Creed (Merfolk).name: Cantrip
Zendikar; Ula Creed (Merfolk).description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

Zendikar; Ula Creed (Merfolk).name: Water Creed Navigation
Zendikar; Ula Creed (Merfolk).description: You have proficiency with navigator's tools and in the Survival skill.

Beasthide (Shifter).name: Natural Athlete
Beasthide (Shifter).description: You have proficiency in the Athletics skill.

Beasthide (Shifter).name: Shifting Feature
Beasthide (Shifter).description: Whenever you shift, you gain 1d6 additional temporary hit points. While shifted, you have a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.

Longtooth (Shifter).name: Fierce
Longtooth (Shifter).description: You have proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

Longtooth (Shifter).name: Shifting Feature
Longtooth (Shifter).description: While shifted, you can use your elongated fangs to make an unarmed strike as a bonus action. If you hit with your fangs, you can deal piercing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

Swiftstride (Shifter).name: Graceful
Swiftstride (Shifter).description: You have proficiency in the Acrobatics skill.

Swiftstride (Shifter).name: Shifting Feature
Swiftstride (Shifter).description: While shifted, your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you can move up to 10 feet as a reaction when a creature ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This reactive movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

Wildhunt (Shifter).name: Natural Tracker
Wildhunt (Shifter).description: You have proficiency in the Survival skill.

Wildhunt (Shifter).name: Shifting Feature
